description: Assign properties to liner elements in the range. The properties consist of constitutive model properties, shell-type properties, and coupling-spring properties. The constitutive model property names are primary keywords, but are not included in the list above. The following properties are available, with the constitutive model properties listed first.

6.0.description: Assigns a property to elements in the range. The element can have isotropic, orthotropic, or anisotropic elastic material properties. Note that if the liner is embedded, then coupling spring properties must be entered separately on side 2. The following properties are available:

keywords.name: density
keywords.description: density (needed if dynamic mode or gravity is active)

keywords.name: thickness
keywords.description: liner thickness

keywords.name: thermal-expansion
keywords.description: thermal expansion coefficient

keywords.name: anisotropic-membrane
keywords.syntax: anisotropic-membrane f1 f2 f3 f4 f5 f6
keywords.description: anisotropic membrane material properties {c’11, c’12, c’13, c’22, c’23, c’33} [F/L2], which define membrane material-stiffness matrices [E’m] and [E’b], respectively, in the material directions x’, y’, z’

keywords.name: anisotropic-bending
keywords.syntax: anisotropic-bending f1 f2 f3 f4 f5 f6
keywords.description: anisotropic bending material properties {c’11, c’12, c’13, c’22, c’23, c’33} [F/L2], which define bending material-stiffness matrices [E’m] and [E’b], respectively, in the material directions x’, y’, z’

keywords.name: anisotropic-both
keywords.syntax: anisotropic-both f1 f2 f3 f4 f5 f6
keywords.description: anisotropic membrane and bending material properties {c’11, c’12, c’13, c’22, c’23, c’33} [F/L2], which define membrane and bending material-stiffness matrices [E’m] and [E’b], respectively, in the material directions x’, y’, z’

keywords.name: material-x
keywords.description: specify the vector v whose projection onto the geogrid surface defines the xʹ-axis of the material coordinate system. The material directions correspond with the principal directions of orthotropy (for more information, see below).

keywords.name: isotropic
keywords.description: isotropic material properties: E and v where E is Young’s modulus [F/L2] and v is Poisson’s ratio

keywords.name: orthotropic-membrane
keywords.description: orthotropic membrane material properties { c’11, c’12, c’22, c’33 } [F/L2], which define membrane material-stiffness matrices [ E’m ] and [ E’b ], respectively, in the material directions x’, y’, z’.

keywords.name: orthotropic-bending
keywords.description: orthotropic bending material properties { c’11, c’12, c’22, c’33 } [F/L2], which define bending material-stiffness matrices [ E’m ] and [ E’b ], respectively, in the material directions x’, y’, z’.

keywords.name: orthotropic-both
keywords.description: orthotropic membrane and bending material properties { c’11, c’12, c’22, c’33 } [F/L2], which define membrane and bending material-stiffness matrices [ E’m ] and [ E’b ], respectively, in the material directions x’, y’, z’.

keywords.name: slide
keywords.description: large-strain sliding flag

keywords.name: slide-tolerance
keywords.description: large-strain sliding tolerance

keywords.name: coupling-yield-normal
keywords.description: normal coupling spring tensile strength (stress units)

keywords.name: coupling-yield-normal-2
keywords.description: normal coupling spring tensile strength on side 2 (stress units)

keywords.name: coupling-stiffness-normal
keywords.description: normal coupling spring stiffness per unit area

keywords.name: coupling-stiffness-normal-2
keywords.description: normal coupling spring stiffness per unit area on side 2

keywords.name: coupling-stiffness-shear
keywords.description: shear coupling spring stiffness per unit area

keywords.name: coupling-stiffness-shear-2
keywords.description: shear coupling spring stiffness per unit area on side 2

keywords.name: coupling-cohesion-shear
keywords.description: shear coupling spring cohesion (stress unit)

keywords.name: coupling-cohesion-shear-2
keywords.description: shear coupling spring cohesion (stress unit) on side 2

keywords.name: coupling-cohesion-shear-residual
keywords.description: shear coupling spring residual cohesion

keywords.name: coupling-cohesion-shear-residual-2
keywords.description: shear coupling spring residual cohesion on side 2

keywords.name: coupling-friction-shear
keywords.description: shear coupling spring friction angle (degrees)

keywords.name: coupling-friction-shear-2
keywords.description: shear coupling spring friction angle (degrees) on side 2

keywords.name: effective
keywords.description: effective stress flag (default to on). If off, then pore-pressures are not removed from the stress normal to the liner when determining shear failure limits.

7.0.description: Assigns a property to elements in the range. The element can have isotropic, orthotropic, or anisotropic elastic material properties. Note that if the liner is embedded, then coupling spring properties must be entered separately on side 2. The following properties are available:

keywords.name: density
keywords.description: density (needed if dynamic mode or gravity is active)

keywords.name: thickness
keywords.description: liner thickness

keywords.name: thermal-expansion
keywords.description: thermal expansion coefficient

keywords.name: anisotropic-membrane
keywords.syntax: anisotropic-membrane f1 f2 f3 f4 f5 f6
keywords.description: anisotropic membrane material properties {\(c^{\prime}_{11}, c^{\prime}_{12}, c^{\prime}_{13}, c^{\prime}_{22}, c^{\prime}_{23}, c^{\prime}_{33}\)} \([F/L^2]\), which define membrane material-stiffness matrices \([Eʹ_m]\) and \([Eʹ_b]\), respectively, in the material directions \(x'\), \(y'\), \(z'\)

keywords.name: anisotropic-bending
keywords.syntax: anisotropic-bending f1 f2 f3 f4 f5 f6
keywords.description: anisotropic bending material properties {\(c^{\prime}_{11}, c^{\prime}_{12}, c^{\prime}_{13}, c^{\prime}_{22}, c^{\prime}_{23}, c^{\prime}_{33}\)} \([F/L^2]\), which define bending material-stiffness matrices \([Eʹ_m]\) and \([Eʹ_b]\), respectively, in the material directions \(x'\), \(y'\), \(z'\).

keywords.name: material-x
keywords.description: specify the vector v whose projection onto the geogrid surface defines the \(xʹ\)-axis of the material coordinate system. The material directions correspond with the principal directions of orthotropy (for more information, see below).

keywords.name: isotropic
keywords.description: isotropic material properties: \(E\) and \(v\) where \(E\) is Young’s modulus \([F/L^2]\) and \(v\) is Poisson’s ratio

keywords.name: orthotropic-membrane
keywords.description: orthotropic membrane material properties {\(c^{\prime}_{11}, c^{\prime}_{12}, c^{\prime}_{22}, c^{\prime}_{33}\)} \([F/L^2]\), which define membrane material-stiffness matrices \([Eʹ_m]\) and \([Eʹ_b]\), respectively, in the material directions \(x\)‘, \(y\)‘, \(z\)‘.

keywords.name: orthotropic-bending
keywords.description: orthotropic bending material properties {\(c^{\prime}_{11}, c^{\prime}_{12}, c^{\prime}_{22}, c^{\prime}_{33}\)} \([F/L^2]\), which define bending material-stiffness matrices \([Eʹ_m]\) and \([Eʹ_b]\), respectively, in the material directions \(x\)‘, \(y\)‘, \(z\)‘.
